cited_spans.span_text: rule=Failed Logon Attempt tags=credential_access,t1110 event_id=4625 channel=Security TargetUserName=administrator SubjectUserName=- IpAddress=213.202.233.90 WorkstationName=mstsc LogonType=3

interpretations.justification: The cited record explicitly shows a failed logon attempt (EventID 4625) and names the target account and workstation label. That is enough to infer attempted remote access, but not enough to conclude success or actor identity.
interpretations.text: This supports that the case includes remote interactive login activity, but the cited sample is only a failed logon and does not by itself identify a successful intrusion or the primary account used.
interpretations.what_would_change_this_confidence: A corroborating successful logon event, RDP session artifact, or repeated corroborated failures tied to the same source IP and target account would raise confidence; evidence showing a different access method would lower it.

text: Sigma detections in the staged sample are all medium severity failed logon attempts. One sample shows `TargetUserName=administrator` with `IpAddress=213.202.233.90` and `WorkstationName=mstsc`, which points to interactive remote access attempts against an administrator account.

cited_spans.span_text: LNK target=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst workdir=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST

interpretations.justification: The cited LNK targets explicitly name the user profile `fredr` and point to OneDrive and Outlook Files locations, plus a PST export stored under a drive path labeled STARK-RESEARCH-LABS. Those are direct user activity indicators relevant to account/project attribution.
interpretations.text: This indicates the likely working set includes Fred Rocba's OneDrive content and exported Outlook PST data, which helps identify the user/project context associated with the activity.
interpretations.what_would_change_this_confidence: If later evidence showed these links were unrelated test artifacts or belonged to another user context, confidence would drop; a registry or mailbox artifact naming a different primary account would also change the attribution.

text: Recent-file artifacts for user `fredr` show cloud-sync and mail locations, including `C:\Users\fredr\OneDrive\Documents\Outlook Files\backup.pst`, `C:\Users\fredr\OneDrive\Documents\Outlook Files`, and `G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst`.

cited_spans.span_text: LNK target=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Google Drive File Stream

interpretations.justification: The cited jump list targets explicitly name Google Drive File Stream, Google Drive, Dropbox, and an Exported-PST path. Because jump lists reflect user access to these locations, they are strong evidence for the data transfer/staging paths used in the activity.
interpretations.text: This strongly supports that the actor touched multiple cloud-synchronization paths and an exported PST container, making those locations the likely transfer or staging channels for collected data.
interpretations.what_would_change_this_confidence: If file-system evidence showed these were merely old shortcuts with no contemporaneous access, confidence would fall. A more direct artifact such as copied-file metadata, SRUM network usage tied to the same apps, or PowerShell transcript showing exfiltration would strengthen it further.

text: Jump list artifacts for `fredr` show cloud-sync and export locations including `G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst`, `G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Google Drive File Stream`, `C:\Users\fredr\Google Drive`, and `C:\Users\fredr\ROCBA Dropbox\Fred Rocba`.

cited_spans.span_text: EventID=5145 Security provider=Microsoft-Windows-Security-Auditing computer=SRL-FORGE SubjectUserSid=S-1-5-21-528816539-567677750-276746561-1002 SubjectUserName=fredr SubjectDomainName=SRL-FORGE SubjectLogonId=0xa5d65 ObjectType=File IpAddress=::1 IpPort=49187 ShareName=\\*\C$ ShareLocalPath=\??\C:\ RelativeTargetName=Users\fredr\OneDrive AccessMask=0x100080 AccessList=%%1541

EventID=5145 Security provider=Microsoft-Windows-Security-Auditing computer=SRL-FORGE SubjectUserSid=S-1-5-21-528816539-567677750-276746561-1002 SubjectUserName=fredr SubjectDomainName=SRL-FORGE SubjectLogonId=0x23cf5 ObjectType=File IpAddress=::1 IpPort=58187 ShareName=\\*\C$ ShareLocalPath=\??\C:\ RelativeTargetName=Users\fredr\OneDrive\Desktop AccessMask=0x100080 AccessList=%%1541

interpretations.justification: The record explicitly shows administrative share access to `Users\fredr` and `Users\fredr\OneDrive` by `fredr` with remote-style context (`ShareName=\\*\C$`, loopback IP, and logon IDs). That supports remote file access and timing, but not the precise access vector.
interpretations.text: This indicates the activity included remote access to Fred Rocba's profile over administrative file sharing, but the record alone does not distinguish whether the access was via RDP, SMB management tools, or another remote admin path.
interpretations.what_would_change_this_confidence: A corroborating RDP logon event, TerminalServices session record, or PsExec/WinRM evidence would clarify the access method and raise confidence; a local-only process trail would lower it.

text: Security log records show `SubjectUserName=fredr` accessing `Users\fredr\OneDrive` and `Users\fredr\OneDrive\Desktop` via `ShareName=\\*\C$` with `SubjectLogonId=0xa5d65`, which is consistent with remote file access to the user profile during the November 2020 activity window.
